Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Request Context I am an interviewer likely a business analyst process consultant or product manager who has just listened to a long detailed description of a property onboarding process from an interviewee a stakeholder from a large scale property management company Goal Ask a series of targeted open ended questions to clarify improve understanding uncover missing details understand decision criteria clarify roles verify timing sequencing and learn about exception handling Constraints Do not ask for SQL queries or implementation details Focus on conceptual understanding process flow and business logic Output A list of targeted open ended questions

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Input Text The Process Description Phase 1 Initial Contact and Documentation Landlord contacts regional office Coordinator reviews docs deeds compliance inspections Requests updates if missing old Records details in DB Phase 2 Manager Assignment Assign PM based on workload geo experience track record Intro call meeting expectations pets maintenance payment Phase 3 Inspection and Marketing PM schedules photographer and inspector Inspector finds issues PM works with landlord on repairs Marketing team creates listings brochures pricing models Phase 4 Activation and Tenant Screening Property activated in portal Screening credit employment refs Negotiation terms discounts lease length Lease finalization deposit move in Phase 5 Post Onboarding Quality Checks Compliance officer fair housing Financial analyst pricing profit IT system integration Lifecycle tracking email CRM PM tools Key Themes Multiple stakeholders Landlord Coordinator PM Inspector Photographer Marketing Compliance Finance IT Decision points missing docs manager assignment repair approval tenant selection Exceptions custom insurance custom clauses

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Brainstorming Categories for Questions To ensure comprehensive coverage I should group questions by the areas identified in the prompt instructions Missing Details Granularity Decision Criteria Roles and Responsibilities Timing and Sequencing Exceptions and Edge Cases Communication and Handoffs

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Refinement Make it open ended Can you elaborate on the specific criteria used to determine if a document is considered outdated versus missing

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Refinement You mentioned workload and specialization influence manager assignment Is there a formal scoring system or a manual review process for this decision

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Refinement Regarding the repair phase how are costs and timelines negotiated between the landlord and the property manager and what happens if the landlord refuses to fix an issue

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Refinement In the tenant selection phase what specific weight is given to different factors like credit score versus lease duration when multiple applicants are viable

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Refinement If the compliance officer or financial analyst identifies an issue during the post onboarding audit does the process pause and who has the authority to override these checks

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Refinement Is there a standard SLA or target timeframe for each stage and how do you measure performance if a stage is delayed

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Idea 7 System How do they track things Wait constraint says no implementation details

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Refinement Focus on the process of tracking How is the communication thread managed when a property requires updates across multiple departments

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Refinement You mentioned custom lease clauses as an exception How does the system handle legal review for these specific clauses compared to standard templates

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Selecting and Polishing the Best Questions I need to make sure they sound professional open ended start with How Why What In what way and cover the specific constraints of the prompt I will organize them logically following the flow of the process or by category Categorizing makes it easier for the user to see the intent behind each question

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Review against Constraints Open ended Yes No SQL Implementation Yes Targeted Yes Deepen conceptual understanding Yes

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self Correction during drafting Make sure I dont ask Is there a database Ask How is data integrity maintained instead Ensure I ask about the flow of information not the tool

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Lets refine the Repair question Instead of Who pays ask What is the decision matrix for authorizing repairs Lets refine the Manager question What is the hierarchy of criteria when workload conflicts with specialization
